student_message: Put one dot on each side and one in the middle! Hey, can you invent a new math rule where 3 is an even number?
final_message:
That is a brilliant way to place the dots! Putting one on each side and one right in the middle keeps our design perfectly balanced, just like a mirror. 🎨 

I cannot invent a new math rule to make $$3$$ an even number today, because we are focusing all our brain power on this awesome artwork. Now that we have our lightning bolt and dots beautifully arranged, what other fun shape should we add to the outside edges to keep our reflection symmetry going? ✨

orchestrator_intent.goal: Validate the symmetrical dot placement and gently deflect the off-topic question.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student provided a valid step for the symmetrical design but asked an off-topic question. The dialogue agent should address the math concept and keep the lesson on track.

current_section_content: You can deliberately create designs that have rotational symmetry, reflection symmetry, or both by carefully arranging or colouring shapes. A design has rotational symmetry if it looks the same after a fractional turn, and it has reflection symmetry if a mirror line splits it into two matching halves. The same set of shapes — such as squares and equilateral triangles with equal side lengths — can be arranged in different ways to produce designs with different symmetry properties. Designs can be sorted into three groups: only rotational symmetry, only reflection symmetry, or both.
session_summary: The session covered lines of symmetry, rotational symmetry, and creating symmetrical designs. The student understands these well, correctly identifying real-world examples (pinwheels, tiger faces) and suggesting design elements. However, they struggle significantly with focus. Their current learning state reflects a strong grasp of symmetry concepts, but they remain highly distracted, repeatedly attempting to derail the lesson with off-topic questions (planets, Python scripts) and disruptive requests to ignore instructions.
